title: Key Points MCQ – All Chapters Mixed (200 Questions)
source: Short Textbook of Anesthesia – Ajay Yadav (6th Edition) – Key Points All Chapters

questions.question: What is the most common cause of pulseless electrical activity (PEA)?
questions.explanation: Hypovolemia (shock) is the most common cause of pulseless electrical activity (PEA).

questions.question: Which inhalational agent is the agent of choice for hepatic patients?
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for hepatic patients while halothane and nitrous oxide should be avoided.

questions.question: What is the rate of cardiac compression recommended during CPR?
questions.explanation: The rate of cardiac compression should be 100–120 compressions/minute but not more than 120.

questions.question: Which is the narrowest part of the airway in children up to the age of 6 years?
questions.explanation: Subglottis is the narrowest part in children up to the age of 6 years, however this does not act as deterrent to use cuffed tubes in children.

questions.question: Which vasopressor is the drug of choice for neurogenic shock?
questions.explanation: Phenylephrine is the vasopressor of choice for neurogenic shock, ephedrine for spinal shock and norepinephrine for septicemic shock.

questions.question: Which muscle relaxant is most commonly implicated in causing malignant hyperthermia?
questions.explanation: Succinylcholine is the most commonly implicated drug while halothane is the most commonly implicated inhalational agent for causing malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: What is the gold standard scoring system to assess the severity of hepatic disease?
questions.explanation: Child-Pugh classification is the gold standard scoring system to assess the severity of hepatic disease and hence the risk assessment.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used circuit for children?
questions.explanation: Jackson Rees (Mapleson F) is the most commonly used semiclosed circuit for children.

questions.question: What is the target core body temperature range for targeted temperature management after cardiac arrest?
questions.explanation: Targeted temperature management means maintaining core body temperature between 32–36°C for 24 hours.

questions.question: Which is the most preferred fluid for maintenance as well as replacement?
questions.explanation: Ringer lactate is the most preferred fluid for maintenance as well as replacement.

questions.question: Which is the most common thermal perturbation seen during anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Hypothermia is the most common thermal perturbation seen during anesthesia.

questions.question: Which gas is most frequently used for laparoscopy?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide is the most frequently used gas for laparoscopy because it is more soluble.

questions.question: What is the surest technique to confirm endotracheal intubation?
questions.explanation: Capnography is the surest technique to confirm intubation.

questions.question: Which inhalational agent is the agent of choice for prolonged surgeries, old age, and day care surgery?
questions.explanation: Desflurane is agent of choice for prolonged surgeries, old age, renal diseases, obesity, day care surgery and shock.

questions.question: What is the leading cause of death in ICU?
questions.explanation: Sepsis is the leading cause of death in ICU.

questions.question: Which local anesthetic is absolutely contraindicated for Bier's block due to high cardiotoxicity?
questions.explanation: Because of high cardiotoxicity bupivacaine is absolutely contraindicated for Bier's block.

questions.question: Which induction agent is the agent of choice for shock?
questions.explanation: Ketamine is the induction agent of choice for shock.

questions.question: Which is the most common postoperative complication of central neuraxial blocks in present day practice?
questions.explanation: Urinary retention is the most common postoperative complication of central neuraxial blocks in present day practice.

questions.question: Which position is preferred for intubation in obese patients?
questions.explanation: As the ramped position provides a superior view of laryngoscopy it is preferred position for intubation for obese patients.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used drug for spinal anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Hyperbaric bupivacaine is the most commonly used drug for spinal.

questions.question: Which is the most common source of nosocomial infections in ICU?
questions.explanation: The most common source of nosocomial infections in ICU are urinary tract infections.

questions.question: What is the most common adverse cardiac event during perioperative period in pediatric patients?
questions.explanation: Bradycardia is the most common adverse cardiac event during perioperative period in pediatric patients.

questions.question: Which monitor is the first scientifically validated monitor to assess the depth of anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Bispectral index (BIS) monitor is the first scientifically validated and commercially available monitor to assess the depth of anesthesia.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used laryngoscope?
questions.explanation: Macintosh is the most commonly used laryngoscope.

questions.question: In which type of hypoxia is oxygen therapy NOT helpful?
questions.explanation: Oxygen therapy is helpful in improving oxygen saturation in all types of hypoxia except histotoxic hypoxia.

questions.question: Which nerve block results in maximum systemic absorption of local anesthetic?
questions.explanation: Maximum systemic absorption has been seen after intercostal nerve block.

questions.question: Which is the most common arrhythmia seen in the perioperative period?
questions.explanation: Tachycardia is the most common arrhythmia seen in perioperative period.

questions.question: What is Entonox?
questions.explanation: Entonox is the mixture of 50% oxygen and 50% nitrous oxide.

questions.question: Which is the drug of choice for treatment of shivering after spinal anesthesia?
questions.explanation: The drug of choice for treatment of shivering is pethidine.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used scale for pain assessment?
questions.explanation: Visual analog scale is the most commonly used scale for pain assessment.

questions.question: Which cranial nerve is most commonly involved after spinal anesthesia?
questions.explanation: 6th is most commonly involved cranial nerve after spinal anesthesia.

questions.question: Which inhalational agent can produce Compound A with sodalime?
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane by reacting with sodalime and barylime can produce compound A.

questions.question: Which is the preferred colloid for a patient with compromised hepatic functions?
questions.explanation: Albumin is the preferred colloid for a patient with compromised hepatic functions.

questions.question: What is the most common cause of death in malignant hyperthermia?
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia induced ventricular fibrillation is the most common cause of death in malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: Which anesthetic technique is the choice for PIH (Pregnancy-Induced Hypertension)?
questions.explanation: Anesthetic technique of choice for PIH is epidural.

questions.question: Which IV agent is the most cardiovascular stable?
questions.explanation: Etomidate is the most cardiovascular stable among all IV agents.

questions.question: Which is the most bothersome complication of supraclavicular block?
questions.explanation: The most bothersome complication of supraclavicular block is pneumothorax.

questions.question: Which is the induction agent of choice for epileptics?
questions.explanation: Thiopentone is the induction agent of choice for epileptics.

questions.question: Which is the most common complication of spinal anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Hypotension is the most common complication of spinal anesthesia.

questions.question: Which muscle relaxant is the nondepolarizer of choice for intubation?
questions.explanation: Rocuronium is nondepolarizer of choice for intubation.

questions.question: What is the most common cause of anesthesia-related death in the perioperative period?
questions.explanation: Postoperative respiratory depression has been cited as the most common cause of anesthesia related death in perioperative period.

questions.question: Which method is the most commonly used technique for painless labor?
questions.explanation: Most commonly used technique for painless labor is continuous lumbar epidural.

questions.question: What is the color of oxygen cylinder?
questions.explanation: The color of oxygen cylinder is black body with white shoulders (top) and pressure is 2,000 pounds per square inch (psi).

questions.question: Which is the most important goal of anesthetic management for patients of ischemic heart disease?

options.B: Avoid imbalance between myocardial oxygen demand and supply

questions.explanation: The most important goal of anesthetic management for patients of ischemic heart disease is to avoid an imbalance between myocardial oxygen demand and supply.

questions.question: Which is the preferred route for postoperative analgesia?
questions.explanation: Epidural route is the most preferred route for postoperative analgesia.

questions.question: Which monitor is considered best to detect intraoperative myocardial infarction?
questions.explanation: Transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) is the earliest and most sensitive monitor to detect intra-operative infarction.

questions.question: What is the most common cause of hypotension in the perioperative period?
questions.explanation: The most common cause of hypotension in perioperative period is intravascular volume depletion.

questions.question: Which is the most sensitive tool to detect venous air embolism?
questions.explanation: The most sensitive tool to detect venous air embolism is transesophageal echocardiography.

questions.question: What depth should cardiac massage depress the sternum during CPR?
questions.explanation: Cardiac massage should be able to depress sternum by 2 inches (5 cm) but not more than 2.4 inches (6 cm).

questions.question: Which is the opioid of choice for day care surgery?
questions.explanation: Remifentanil is the opioid of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used method to locate epidural space?
questions.explanation: Most commonly used method to locate epidural space is by loss of resistance technique.

questions.question: Which inhalational agent is the agent of choice for induction in children?
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane is the agent of choice for induction in children.

questions.question: Which is the most common cause of laryngospasm in anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Secretions is the most common cause of laryngospasm in anesthesia.

questions.question: Which drug is the drug of choice for neuropathic pain?
questions.explanation: Drug of choice for neuropathic pain is pregabalin followed by gabapentin.

questions.question: How much does one unit of blood/packed cells increase hemoglobin?
questions.explanation: One unit of blood/packed cells increases hemoglobin by 0.8%.

questions.question: What is the triad of fat embolism syndrome?
questions.explanation: The triad of fat embolism syndrome is dyspnea, confusion and petechial hemorrhages. Petechial rash is pathognomonic of FES.

questions.question: Which artery is most commonly chosen for arterial cannulation?
questions.explanation: As radial artery has collaterals (transpalmar arch) for the blood supply of hand, it is most commonly chosen artery for cannulation.

questions.question: Which is the most common postoperative pulmonary complication?
questions.explanation: Atelectasis is the most common postoperative pulmonary complication and the most common cause of atelectasis is secretions.

questions.question: Which is the induction agent of choice for electroconvulsive therapy?
questions.explanation: Methohexital is the induction agent of choice for electroconvulsive therapy.

questions.question: After cerebrovascular accident, for how long should elective surgery be deferred?
questions.explanation: After cerebrovascular accident elective surgery should be deferred for 9 months.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used muscle for neuromuscular monitoring?
questions.explanation: Most commonly used muscle for neuromuscular monitoring is adductor pollicis supplied by ulnar nerve.

questions.question: What is the most common cause of nausea and vomiting after spinal anesthesia?
questions.explanation: The most common cause of nausea and vomiting is hypotension.

questions.question: Which is the most common cause of death after massive transfusion?
questions.explanation: DIC is the most common cause of death after massive transfusion while acute lung injury remains the leading cause of mortality after transfusion.

questions.question: Which is the most preferred vasopressor for treatment of spinal hypotension?
questions.explanation: Ephedrine is the most preferred vasopressor for the treatment of spinal hypotension.

questions.question: Minimum alveolar concentration (MAC) decreases by what percentage in pregnant patients?
questions.explanation: Minimum alveolar concentration decreases by 25 to 40% in pregnant patients making them more susceptible to anesthetic overdosage.

questions.question: Which is the most cardiovascular stable muscle relaxant?
questions.explanation: Vecuronium is most cardiovascular stable muscle relaxant.

questions.question: Which is the inhalational agent of choice for asthma patients?
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for asthma patients.

questions.question: The standard sequence of CPR was changed to which order in 2010 guidelines?
questions.explanation: The standard sequence of CPR, i.e. A (airway) → B (breathing) → C (circulation) was changed to C → A → B in 2010 CPR guidelines.

questions.question: Which is the most common cause of anaphylaxis in anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Muscle relaxants are the most common cause of anaphylaxis followed by latex allergy.

questions.question: What is the ideal PEEP range in current day critical care practice?
questions.explanation: Ideal PEEP is the PEEP which maintains oxygen saturation >90% without decreasing the cardiac output significantly. Usually, it is between 5–12 cm H2O.

questions.question: Which muscle relaxant is the choice for day care surgery?
questions.explanation: Mivacurium is the muscle relaxant of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: Which inhalational agent is absolutely contraindicated in pheochromocytoma?
questions.explanation: Halothane is absolutely contraindicated in pheochromocytoma because it sensitizes myocardium to catecholamines.

questions.question: Which is the most important predisposing factor for aspiration?
questions.explanation: Full stomach is the single most important predisposing factor for aspiration.

questions.question: Which inhalational agent is the agent of choice for cardiac patients?
questions.explanation: Isoflurane is the agent of choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: What is the most common cause of metabolic acidosis in anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Lactic acidosis is the most common cause of metabolic acidosis in anesthesia and is due to tissue hypoxia.

questions.question: What is the fresh gas flow requirement for Bain circuit during controlled ventilation?
questions.explanation: Bain circuit is the circuit of choice for controlled ventilation. Fresh gas flow for controlled ventilation is 1.6 times of minute ventilation.

questions.question: Which nondepolarizer is the only one that can cross the placenta?
questions.explanation: Gallamine is the only nondepolarizer which can cross the placenta therefore absolutely contraindicated in pregnancy.

questions.question: What percentage of fires in OT are caused by cautery?
questions.explanation: Cautery is responsible for 68% of fires seen in OT.

questions.question: Which IV agent is the agent of choice for day care surgery?
questions.explanation: Propofol is the IV agent of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: What is the maximum duration the tourniquet should NOT exceed for both upper and lower limbs?
questions.explanation: Duration should not exceed more than 2 hours for both upper and lower limbs.

questions.question: Which approach is best utilized for carotid endarterectomy?
questions.explanation: Cervical plexus block is best utilized for carotid endarterectomy.

questions.question: What is the recommended tidal volume for ARDS management?
questions.explanation: Low tidal volume (6 mL/kg), airway plateau pressure < 30 cm H2O and low FiO2 (<0.5) are the key in the management of ARDS.

questions.question: Which is the most potent analgesic among IV agents?
questions.explanation: Ketamine is the most potent analgesic among IV agents.

questions.question: In which position does aspiration most commonly involve the apical segment of right lower lobe?
questions.explanation: Aspiration in supine position most commonly involves apical segment of right lower lobe.

questions.question: Which is the most common organism responsible for meningitis after spinal anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Streptococcus viridans has been cited as the most common organism responsible for meningitis after spinal while Staphylococcus epidermidis is the most common organism responsible for meningitis after epidural.

questions.question: In obese patients, lipid soluble drugs should be dosed on which body weight?
questions.explanation: Lipid soluble drugs (IV induction agents, opioids) have increased volume of distribution, therefore given on actual body weight basis.

questions.question: Which is the gold standard technique for management of difficult/failed intubation?
questions.explanation: Fiberoptic laryngoscope/bronchoscope-guided intubation is considered as gold standard technique for the management of difficult/failed intubation.

questions.question: Which is the induction agent of choice for cardiac patients?
questions.explanation: Etomidate is the induction agent of choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: Which is the most common postoperative complication of laparoscopic surgeries?
questions.explanation: Nausea and vomiting is the most common postoperative complication of laparoscopic surgeries; incidence can be as high as 40–75%.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used modality for neuromuscular monitoring in clinical practice?
questions.explanation: Train of four is the most commonly used modality for neuromuscular monitoring in clinical practice.

questions.question: What is the maximum safe dose of lignocaine without adrenaline?
questions.explanation: Maximum safe dose of lignocaine without adrenaline is 4.5 mg/kg (maximum 300 mg) while with adrenaline it is 7 mg/kg (maximum 500 mg).

questions.question: What is the end point to stop CPR?
questions.explanation: The end point to stop CPR is the return of spontaneous circulation (ROSC) assessed by palpating carotids.

questions.question: Which blood products are stored at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Platelets are the only blood products which are stored at room temperature.

questions.question: Which is the most common cardiac finding in old age patients?
questions.explanation: Diastolic dysfunction, the most common cardiac finding in old age, is a significant determinant of morbidity.

questions.question: Which newer reversal agent rapidly reverses the block by steroidal nondepolarizers?
questions.explanation: Sugammadex is the newer reversal agent which rapidly and completely reverses the block by steroidal type of nondepolarizers but cannot reverse benzylisoquinoline compounds.

questions.question: What is the leading cause of mortality after blood transfusion?
questions.explanation: DIC is the most common cause of death after massive transfusion while acute lung injury remains the leading cause of mortality after transfusion.

questions.question: Which is the preferred inhalational agent for neurosurgery in current day practice?
questions.explanation: Due to more favorable effects sevoflurane is preferred over isoflurane as an inhalational agent of choice in neurosurgery in current day anesthetic practice.

questions.question: What is the compression to breathing ratio for adult CPR without advanced airway (single rescuer)?
questions.explanation: Without advanced airway the ratio of compression to breathing for adults should be 30:2 irrespective of number of resuscitators.

questions.question: Which is the inhalational agent of choice for maintenance in obese patients?
questions.explanation: Desflurane is the inhalational agent of choice for maintenance in obese patients.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used IV anesthetic for induction in current day practice?
questions.explanation: Most commonly used IV anesthetic for induction in current day practice is propofol.

questions.question: What is the most common cause of mismatch transfusion?
questions.explanation: Most common cause of mismatch transfusion is ABO incompatibility.

questions.question: Which vein is the vein of choice for parenteral nutrition?
questions.explanation: Subclavian is vein of choice for parenteral nutrition.

questions.question: Which agent is inhibited by succinylcholine from being used in burns for one year?
questions.explanation: In burns succinylcholine is contraindicated for one year while dose of nondepolarizers is increased.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used device to preserve humidity in anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Heat and moisture exchanger (HME) is the most commonly used device to preserve humidity in anesthesia.

questions.question: What should be the target cerebral perfusion pressure during neurosurgical procedures?
questions.explanation: The aim during neurosurgical procedures is to maintain cerebral perfusion pressure between 70 and 100 mm Hg.

questions.question: Which is the most preferred approach to achieve analgesia for thoracic and upper abdominal surgeries?
questions.explanation: Thoracic epidural is the most preferred approach to achieve analgesia for thoracic and upper abdominal surgeries while parenteral opioids should be avoided as much as possible.

questions.question: What is the most bothersome complication of posterior fossa craniotomies?
questions.explanation: Venous air embolism is the most bothersome complication of posterior fossa craniotomies.

questions.question: Which circuit is the circuit of choice for spontaneous ventilation?
questions.explanation: Magill circuit is the circuit of choice for spontaneous ventilation.

questions.question: Which is the most widely used technique for autologous blood donation?
questions.explanation: Normovolemic/isovolemic hemodilution is the most widely used technique for autologous blood donation.

questions.question: What is the minimum hemoglobin considered optimal for elective surgery?
questions.explanation: Minimum hemoglobin of 8 g/dL is considered optimal to accept the patient for elective surgery.

questions.question: Which second generation LMA is most frequently used in India?
questions.explanation: Among the second generation LMA, I-Gel is most frequently used in India.

questions.question: What should be the pCO2 target for optimal hypocapnia in neurosurgery?
questions.explanation: pCO2 should be maintained between 25–30 mm Hg to achieve optimal hypocapnia.

questions.question: Which is the most common CNS complication in the perioperative period?
questions.explanation: Delayed recovery is the most common CNS complication.

questions.question: Which is the induction agent of choice for hyperthyroid patients?
questions.explanation: Thiopentone is the induction agent of choice in hyperthyroid patients.

questions.question: Which opioid receptor is the most important for the action of opioids?
questions.explanation: The most important receptors for the action of opioids are mu receptors.

questions.question: At what intra-abdominal pressure can renal and mesenteric blood flow be significantly impaired during laparoscopy?
questions.explanation: Intra-abdominal pressure of > 20 mm Hg can significantly impair renal, mesenteric and intestinal blood flow.

questions.question: Which drug still responds best to carbamazepine for treatment?
questions.explanation: Trigeminal neuralgia still responds best to carbamazepine.

questions.question: Which is the most common postoperative nerve injury associated with general anesthesia?
questions.explanation: Brachial plexus injuries are the most common postoperative nerve injury associated with general anesthesia followed by ulnar nerve.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used video laryngoscope?
questions.explanation: Most commonly used video laryngoscopes is C-MAC.

questions.question: Which drug can produce vasopressin resistant polyuric renal failure?
questions.explanation: Methoxyflurane can produce vasopressin resistant polyuric renal failure.

questions.question: Which is the muscle relaxant of choice for cardiac patients?
questions.explanation: Vecuronium is the muscle relaxant of choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: Successful cardiac massage should produce ETCO2 of at least how much?
questions.explanation: Successful cardiac massage should be able to produce ETCO2 of at least 20 mm Hg.

questions.question: Which is the most important preventive measure to prevent post spinal headache?

options.C: Use of small gauge and dura separating needles

questions.explanation: Use of small gauge and dura separating needles are the most important preventive measures to prevent post spinal headache.

questions.question: Which is the best guide to assess tissue perfusion (cardiac output)?

options.C: Oxygen saturation of mixed venous blood

questions.explanation: Oxygen saturation of mixed venous blood is best guide to assess tissue perfusion (cardiac output).

questions.question: Which benzodiazepine is the choice for day-care patients?
questions.explanation: Midazolam is the benzodiazepine of choice for day-care patients.

questions.question: Central neuraxial blocks are absolutely contraindicated in which condition?
questions.explanation: Central neuraxial blocks are absolutely contraindicated in raised ICT.

questions.question: What is the mortality rate of CO2 embolism in laparoscopy?
questions.explanation: CO2 embolism can be fatal complication of laparoscopy; it carries a mortality of 30%.

questions.question: For how long should smoking ideally be stopped before surgery?
questions.explanation: Ideally smoking should be stopped 8 weeks before surgery however stopping smoking 12 hours before may be beneficial by reducing carboxyhemoglobin levels.

questions.question: Which leads are most preferred for ECG monitoring in anesthesia?
questions.explanation: The most preferred leads for ECG monitoring are lead II and V5. Arrhythmia are best detected in lead II and ischemia's in lead V5.

questions.question: For which condition is exchange transfusion NOT practiced in current day?
questions.explanation: Exchange transfusion is not practiced technique for sickle cell patient in current day. The approach is just to correct the anemia by giving red cell transfusion with the target hematocrit > 30.

questions.question: Which is the preferred method of ventilation during CPR?
questions.explanation: Intubation is the most definitive and best method for ventilation.

questions.question: Which is the inhalational agent of choice for renal patients?
questions.explanation: Desflurane is the inhalational agent of choice because it does not produce fluoride.

questions.question: What is the recommended target plasma sugar level in the perioperative period?
questions.explanation: The target plasma sugar level should be between 150–180 mg/dL in perioperative period.

questions.question: Which is the only benzodiazepine used in the intraoperative period?
questions.explanation: Midazolam is the only BZ used in intraoperative period.

questions.question: Bag and mask ventilation is contraindicated in which pediatric condition?
questions.explanation: Bag and mask ventilation is contraindicated for diaphragmatic hernia, tracheoesophageal fistula and pyloric stenosis.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly performed block after central neuraxial block?
questions.explanation: Brachial plexus block is the second most commonly performed block (after central neuraxial block).

questions.question: What shock energy should be used with biphasic defibrillators?
questions.explanation: Shock energy should be 360J with monophasic defibrillators and 150–200 with biphasic defibrillators.

questions.question: Which is the most prominent effect of suxamethonium?
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia is the most prominent effect of suxamethonium.

questions.question: How long should MAO-A inhibitors be stopped before surgery?
questions.explanation: MAO-A inhibitors should be stopped 3 weeks before surgery.

questions.question: Which is the most definitive modality of treatment for facet arthropathy?
questions.explanation: Radiofrequency (RF) ablation of medial branch of posterior ramus of spinal nerve is the most definitive modality of treatment for facet arthropathy.

questions.question: Which is the simplest method to decrease ventilator-associated pneumonia?
questions.explanation: Positing head at 30 degrees proves to be simplest and most cost effective method to decrease ventilator associated pneumonia.

questions.question: Which agent is used for inhibiting stress response to laryngoscopy and intubation?
questions.explanation: Sufentanil is the agent of choice for inhibiting stress response to laryngoscopy and intubation.

questions.question: For pregnant patients, which position prevents supine hypotension syndrome?
questions.explanation: To prevent SHS the pregnant patient should lie in left lateral position.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used technique for most laparoscopic surgeries?

options.D: GA with endotracheal intubation and mild hyperventilation

questions.explanation: General anesthesia with endotracheal intubation and mild hyperventilation is the most commonly used technique for most of the laparoscopic surgeries.

questions.question: What is the most important complication of laser surgery in ENT?
questions.explanation: The most important complication of laser surgery is fires.

questions.question: Which is the most preferred inhalational agent for geriatric patients?
questions.explanation: Desflurane because of its rapid emergence and least metabolism is the preferred inhalational agent for geriatric patients.

questions.question: What is the most common cause of death in perioperative period in diabetic patients?
questions.explanation: Myocardial ischemia is the most common cause of death in perioperative period in diabetic patients.

questions.question: Which is the most potent inhalational agent?
questions.explanation: Halothane is the most potent while nitrous oxide is the least potent among the agents used nowadays.

questions.question: After which drug is Wooden chest syndrome (muscle rigidity) most commonly seen?
questions.explanation: Muscle rigidity (Wooden chest syndrome) is most commonly seen with alfentanil.

questions.question: What should be the minimum platelet count before considering CNB?
questions.explanation: Patients with platelet count more than 80,000/cubic mm can be safely given central neuraxial blocks.

questions.question: Which is the induction agent of choice for right to left shunts?
questions.explanation: As ketamine increases the systemic vascular resistance it is agent of choice for induction in right to left shunts.

questions.question: What is the major side effect of etomidate?
questions.explanation: The major side effect of etomidate is adrenocortical suppression.

questions.question: How long should Clopidogrel be stopped before CNB?
questions.explanation: CNB should be delayed for 7 days after clopidogrel.

questions.question: Which is the most preferred inhalational agent for maintenance in myasthenia gravis?
questions.explanation: Desflurane is the most preferred inhalational agent for maintenance in myasthenia gravis patients because it produces maximum muscle relaxation among the currently used inhalational agents.

questions.question: What is the combination of choice for TIVA in day-care surgery?
questions.explanation: The combination of choice for TIVA is Propofol + Remifentanil.

questions.question: Which local anesthetic can produce methemoglobinemia?
questions.explanation: Methemoglobinemia is usually seen with prilocaine however benzocaine can also cause methemoglobinemia.

questions.question: Which nerve is usually spared with interscalene approach of brachial plexus block?
questions.explanation: Ulnar nerve is usually spared with interscalene approach while musculocutaneous is usually spared with axillary approach.

questions.question: What is the most common neuropathy encountered in clinical practice?
questions.explanation: Most common neuropathy encountered in clinical practice is diabetic neuropathy.

questions.question: Which is the safest, cheapest and only complete anesthetic among inhalational agents?
questions.explanation: Ether is safest, cheapest and only complete anesthetic while on flip side, it is highly inflammable and explosive and induction and recovery are very unpleasant.

questions.question: What is the most reliable parameter to assess fluid status and titrate fluid therapy?
questions.explanation: Stroke volume variation is considered as most reliable to assess fluid status and titrate fluid therapy.

questions.question: Allergic reactions are common with which type of local anesthetics?
questions.explanation: Allergic reactions are common with esters but rare with amides.

questions.question: For how long should elective surgery be deferred after bare metal stent placement?
questions.explanation: Elective surgery must be deferred for 6 weeks if bare metal stent was used.

questions.question: Which is the most common complication in the postoperative period?
questions.explanation: Pain is the most common complication is postoperative period.

questions.question: Unwitnessed cardiac arrest in adults should be considered due to which rhythm until proved otherwise?
questions.explanation: Unwitnessed cardiac arrest in adults should be considered due to ventricular fibrillation until proved otherwise and in children should be considered due to asystole until proved otherwise.

questions.question: Which is the first system involved in local anesthetic toxicity?
questions.explanation: Central nervous system is the first system involved in local anesthetic toxicity.

questions.question: Which is the drug of choice for treatment of bupivacaine-induced ventricular tachycardia?
questions.explanation: Amiodarone now a days is considered as drug of choice for the treatment of bupivacaine (and other local anesthetic) induced ventricular tachycardia.

questions.question: Which is considered most ideal gas for laparoscopy?
questions.explanation: Argon is considered as most ideal gas for laparoscopy.

questions.question: What is the mode of choice for ventilation in current day critical care practice?
questions.explanation: Pressure regulated volume control (PRVC) can be considered as mode of choice for ventilation in current day critical care practice.

questions.question: At what pH should soda bicarbonate be used to treat acidosis?
questions.explanation: Soda bicarbonate should be used to treat only severe acidosis (pH <7.15).

questions.question: For solid food, how many hours of fasting is required before surgery?
questions.explanation: For solid food 8 hours of fasting is must while clear fluids can be given up to 2 hours.

questions.question: Which is the only recommended method for brain protection after cardiac arrest?
questions.explanation: Targeted temperature management is the only recommended method for brain protection.

questions.question: Which local anesthetic has significant extrahepatic metabolism?
questions.explanation: Prilocaine has significant extrahepatic metabolism.

questions.question: Which relaxants are the choice for hepatic and renal failure?
questions.explanation: Atracurium and cisatracurium are metabolized by Hoffman degradation making them relaxant of choice for hepatic and renal failure.

questions.question: What is the two most common causes of unexpected admission after day care surgery?
questions.explanation: The two most common causes of unexpected admission are uncontrolled pain and nausea and vomiting.

questions.question: Intra-abdominal pressure of 12–14 mm Hg can decrease the cardiac output by how much?
questions.explanation: Intra-abdominal pressure of 12–14 mm Hg can decrease the cardiac output by 30–35%.

questions.question: Which condition can precipitate multiple sclerosis?
questions.explanation: Spinal anesthesia (but not epidural) and stress can precipitate multiple sclerosis; peripheral blocks can be given safely.

questions.question: Which is the drug of choice for postoperative nausea and vomiting?
questions.explanation: Ondansetron/Granisetron remains the drugs of choice for treatment as well as prophylaxis for postoperative nausea and vomiting.

questions.question: Which muscle relaxant is absolutely contraindicated for muscular dystrophies?
questions.explanation: Succinylcholine is absolutely contraindicated for muscular dystrophies; it may produce cardiac arrest due to hyperkalemia and rhabdomyolysis and can precipitate malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: The line joining the highest point on iliac crest corresponds to which vertebral level?
questions.explanation: The line joining the highest point on iliac crest corresponds to L4 and L5 interspace or L4 spine.

questions.question: What is the MAC requirement in decreasing order?
questions.explanation: MAC requirement in decreasing order is infants > neonates > adults.

questions.question: Which is the most commonly used agent to produce controlled hypotension?
questions.explanation: Nitroglycerin as continuous infusion is the most commonly used agent to produce controlled hypotension.

questions.question: Which technique is considered gold standard monitoring of blood pressure?
questions.explanation: Invasive blood pressure monitoring is considered as gold standard monitoring of blood pressure.

questions.question: What is the optimal intra-abdominal pressure range for laparoscopy?
questions.explanation: Intra-abdominal pressure should be kept between 12 and 14 mm Hg.

questions.question: Which is considered safe for giving central neuraxial blocks in patients on antiplatelet therapy?
questions.explanation: Patients on aspirin and NSAIDs can be safely given central neuraxial blocks while CNB should be delayed for 7 days after clopidogrel.

questions.question: Which drug was removed from 2015 CPR guidelines?
questions.explanation: Vasopressin, which was given after 1st dose of adrenaline, has been removed from 2015 CPR guidelines.

questions.question: Which is the shortest acting local anesthetic?
questions.explanation: Chloroprocaine is the shortest acting and dibucaine is the longest acting local anesthetic.

questions.question: What is the blood sugar target for neurosurgical patients?
questions.explanation: Blood sugar should be best maintained between 140–180 mg%.

questions.question: Which is the most common cause of airway obstruction in unconscious patients?
questions.explanation: Epiglottis, rather than tongue fall, has been found to be the more important cause of airway obstruction in unconscious patient.

questions.question: What is the incidence of fatal PE after hip surgery?
questions.explanation: The incidence of fatal PE after hip surgery can be as high as 1–3%.

questions.question: Which is the induction agent of choice for hepatic patients?
questions.explanation: Propofol, due to its short half-life and extrahepatic metabolism is the induction agent of choice for hepatic patients.

questions.question: Which is the most common airway used to prevent tongue fall?
questions.explanation: The most common airway used to prevent the tongue fall is Guedel's airway.

questions.question: What should be done regarding antiepileptic drugs before surgery?
questions.explanation: All antiepileptic should be continued.

questions.question: What determines the flow rate in rotameter?
questions.explanation: Upper end of bobbin determines the flow rate in rotameter.

questions.question: Which is the preferred crystalloid for shock?
questions.explanation: Crystalloids are preferred over colloids for shock and Ringer is the most preferred crystalloid.

questions.question: Which block has almost completely replaced retrobulbar block for eye surgery?
questions.explanation: Peribulbar block has now almost completely replaced retrobulbar block.

questions.question: After which duration should elective surgery be deferred in patient with URTI?
questions.explanation: If surgery has to be deferred in a patient of URTI then it should ideally be deferred for 6 weeks.

questions.question: What is the overall prevalence of postoperative delirium in older patients?
questions.explanation: The overall prevalence of postoperative delirium in older patients is estimated to be 10%.

questions.question: How long should prognostication be delayed after cardiac arrest?
questions.explanation: Prognostication is recommended after 72 hours.

questions.question: Which is the main aim of blood transfusion?
questions.explanation: The main aim of blood transfusion is to improve the oxygen delivery of tissues, not the volume replacement (which should be done by fluids).

questions.question: Which is the method of choice if opioids have to be given by parenteral route for postoperative analgesia?
questions.explanation: Patient-controlled analgesia (PCA) is the method of choice if opioids have to be given by parenteral route.
